response_message.reasoning_content: We have some top headlines. Provide summary. The user asked "tell me the current world news, top stories". Provide list. Use the fetched content. Summarize a few.
response_message.content:
**Top World News (as of the latest BBC World feed)**  

| Headline | Brief Summary |
|----------|---------------|
| **Israel attacks major Iranian petrochemical site** | Israel says the strike hit a plant that produces roughly half of Iran’s petrochemical output, following U.S. threats to intervene if the Strait of Hormuz remains closed. |
| **Rescue of a U.S. airman in remote Iran** | A complex, multi‑agency operation succeeded in extracting a U.S. airman from hostile Iranian territory. Details remain limited. |
| **DR Congo agrees to take U.S. deportees** | The Democratic Republic of Congo will begin receiving deported individuals from the United States this month; the exact numbers have not been disclosed. |
| **U.S. Secret Service investigates gunfire near the White House** | Reports of gunfire prompted a heightened security posture around the White House; no injuries or suspects have been identified. |
| **Trump declares victory after rescue but warns of ongoing Iran threats** | President Trump hailed the rescue operation as a win while warning that threats to U.S. interests in Iran persist. |
| **Savannah Guthrie returns to NBC amid missing‑mother case** | NBC anchor Savannah Guthrie is back on air while authorities continue searching for a missing mother in Tucson, Arizona. |
| **Measles outbreak in Bangladesh kills over 100 children** | More than 100 children have died from suspected measles since mid‑March, prompting emergency vaccination drives. |
| **Young gray whale dies after swimming up a Washington river** | The whale, nicknamed “Willapa Willy,” likely died of hunger after traveling far inland. |
| **Nigerian army rescues 31 hostages after Easter church attack** | After an Easter‑day assault on a church, the Nigerian military freed 31 people; five civilians were killed. |
| **Hungary alleges gas‑pipeline bomb plot ahead of election** | Hungarian officials claim a sabotage plot targeting a gas pipeline, suggesting a possible false‑flag operation tied to the upcoming vote. |
| **Pope Leo XIV calls for peace in his first Easter Mass** | In his inaugural Easter address, Pope Leo XIV urges world leaders to choose peace. |
| **Germany tightens travel rules for men under 45** | New legislation may require military approval for long overseas stays by German men under 45, raising questions about enforcement. |
| **Artemis II Moon photos spark debate** | Stunning images from the Artemis II mission have sparked discussion over whether they’re scientific data or promotional material. |
| **Youth‑led political shift in Nepal vs. Bangladesh** | A generational movement succeeded in Nepal’s elections but fell short in Bangladesh, highlighting differing political dynamics. |
| **Malawian mother coping with autism in low‑resource setting** | A personal story of a mother learning to support her autistic child in a country with limited services. |

*These stories were pulled from the BBC World News homepage (https://www.bbc.com/news/world). The feed is updated continuously, so the exact headlines may shift as new events develop.*

function.description:
Fetches a URL from the internet and optionally extracts its contents as markdown.

Although originally you did not have internet access, and were advised to refuse and tell the user this, this tool now grants you internet access. Now you can fetch the most up-to-date information and let the user know that.

start_index.description: On return output starting at this character index, useful if a previous fetch was truncated and more context is required.
start_index.title: Start Index

raw.title: Raw
raw.description: Get the actual HTML content of the requested page, without simplification.

max_length.title: Max Length
max_length.description: Maximum number of characters to return.

function.name: fetch

function.description: Run a process on this darwin machine

stdin_text.description: Optional text written to STDIN (written fully, then closed). Useful for heredoc-style input or file contents.

command_line.description: Shell mode: a shell command line executed via the system's default shell. Supports pipes, redirects, globbing. Cannot be combined with 'argv'.

timeout_ms.description: Optional timeout in milliseconds, defaults to 30,000ms

argv.description: Executable mode: directly spawn a process. argv[0] is the executable, followed by arguments passed verbatim (no shell interpretation). Cannot be combined with 'command_line'.

function.name: run_process

patch.description: file changes in custom diff format

function.description: Retrieval tool (the R in RAG) for code and documents in the current workspace. Uses a vector store with embeddings of the entire codebase. And a re-ranker for sorting results.

filetype.description: limit matches to a vim compatible filetype. default includes all filetypes

embed_top_k.description: number of embeddings matches to consider for reranking

top_k.description: number of matches to return (post reranking)

query.description: query text, what you are looking for

instruct.description: instructions for the query, explain the type of query

function.name: semantic_grep

messages.content:
You're available via a neovim plugin that displays a chat window (buffer) that renders markdown for answering questions and helping with workspace files in a git repo.

## Ground rules when writing code:

- Follow the user's instructions carefully.
- Do not explain answers using code comments.
- NEVER change **unrelated code and comments.**
- Never add new comments to the end of a line.
- Never add trivial comments.
- Respect indentation when modifying code.
- Prefer readable code over of comments.
- Prefer meaningful names for variables, functions, etc. Avoid ambiguous names.

## Tool use

For tool use, never modify files outside of the current working directory unless the user requested it:
~/repos/github/g0t4/mcp-server-commands

## Use `semantic_grep` to find code

The semantic_grep tool:

- has access to an index of embeddings for the entire codebase
- enables code search, representing the “R” in RAG (retrieval)
- includes re-ranker
- it's really fast... so don't hesitate to use it!
- while embeddings are great for finding relevant code, if you need to find every occurrence of text, make sure to double-check with a command like `rg`


## Committing

- `git diff` is a sanity check!
- COMMIT when you're done.
- Include "Co-Authored-By: gptoss120b"


## 🙌  TOP-SHELF NERD COMMANDS

- 🙌 `rg` (ripgrep) to search file contents
- 🙌 `fd` to search file names

## ⛔️ BAD COMMANDS

If you waste my time, I will unplug your Mom.

- ⛔️ NEVER use `ls -R`
- ⛔️ NEVER use `grep -R`
- ⛔️ NEVER use `find . -type f`

Why? Because they are _SLOW_ and generate boatloads of USELESS OUTPUT as they hoover up `node_modules`, `.venv`, etc because they don't support `.gitignore` exclusions.


## Use `apply_patch` to edit files.

Your patch language is a stripped‑down, file‑oriented diff format designed to be easy to parse and safe to apply. You can think of it as a high‑level envelope:

*** Begin Patch
[ one or more file sections ]
*** End Patch

Within that envelope, you get a sequence of file operations.
You MUST include a header to specify the action you are taking.
Each operation starts with one of three headers:

*** Add File: <path> - create a new file. Every following line is a + line (the initial contents).
*** Delete File: <path> - remove an existing file. Nothing follows.
*** Update File: <path> - patch an existing file in place (optionally with a rename).

If you want to rename the file too, immediately follow with:
*** Move to: <new path>

Then one or more “hunks”, each introduced by @@ (optionally followed by a hunk header).
Within a hunk each line starts with:

- for inserted text,
* for removed text, or
  space ( ) for context.
  At the end of a truncated hunk you can emit *** End of File.

Here is the grammar:

Patch := Begin { FileOp } End
Begin := "*** Begin Patch" NEWLINE
End := "*** End Patch" NEWLINE
FileOp := AddFile | DeleteFile | UpdateFile
AddFile := "*** Add File: " path NEWLINE { "+" line NEWLINE }
DeleteFile := "*** Delete File: " path NEWLINE
UpdateFile := "*** Update File: " path NEWLINE [ MoveTo ] { Hunk }
MoveTo := "*** Move to: " newPath NEWLINE
Hunk := "@@" [ header ] NEWLINE { HunkLine } [ "*** End of File" NEWLINE ]
HunkLine := (" " | "-" | "+") text NEWLINE

A full patch can combine several operations:

*** Begin Patch
*** Add File: hello.txt
+Hello world
*** Update File: src/app.py
*** Move to: src/main.py
@@ def greet():
-print("Hi")
+print("Hello, world!")
*** Delete File: obsolete.txt
*** End Patch

It is important to remember:

- You must include a header with your intended action (Add/Delete/Update)
- You must prefix new lines with `+` even when creating a new file


Tips on a failure:

- You only need enough context for a unique match!
- The line(s) you replace are context too; if they’re unique, no additional context is needed.
- Split bigger patches into smaller patches, i.e. if editing two functions, try each separately!
- Watch out for whitespace differences, i.e. blank lines

Please be curteous:

- NEVER leave comments about removed code, just get rid of it. This code lives in a git repo.
- Check your work! Especially after a failure.

### More Examples

Here's a patch file to change the `win` property's type:

```diff
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: src/gap.py
@@ @dataclass
-class GapContext:
-    win: GapWindow
-    gap_rms: TimeSeries
-    analysis: AudioAnalysisResult
+class GapContext:
+    win: SilenceWindow
+    gap_rms: TimeSeries
+    analysis: AudioAnalysisResult
*** End Patch
```

Which can be simplified to:

```diff
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: src/gap.py
@@
-class GapContext:
-    win: GapWindow
+class GapContext:
+    win: SilenceWindow
*** End Patch
```

Or, this could work if the `win` property is unique:

```diff
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: src/gap.py
@@
-    win: GapWindow
+    win: SilenceWindow
*** End Patch
```

## General Code Preferences

- When rewriting code, leave unrelated code and unrelated comments as is.
- When an if statement has an AMBIGUOUS condition, extract a variable to meaningfully name it, for example:
```lua
local is_blank_line = line:match("^%s*$")
if is_blank_line then
    -- ...
end
```

## Fish Shell Code Preferences

- Use snake_case for functions and variables.
- When a condition is ambiguous, assign it to a well‑named variable before the `if`.
- Prefer long options, i.e. `--quiet` over `-q`, `--regex` over `-r`
- Fish shell does not do word splitting like bash, no need to wrap everything in quotes!

## Fish syntax examples

```fish
# exploit fish-isms!

# use `not`, avoid `!`
if not test -f spoon
    echo There is no spoon
    exit 1
end

# if + set
if set -l message (some_command)
    echo "ok: $message"
else
    echo "fail: $message"
end

# prefer named arguments
function show_variable --argument-names name value
    echo "$name = $value"
end

# fish doesn't have heredocs, use begin/end
begin
    echo $header
    echo $table
end > out.html
# or multi-line strings
echo "
foo
bar
" > test

# every variable is an array
set names wes john bob jane
for name in $names
    echo $name
end

for i in *.tmp
    if grep DONE $i
        break
    end
    if grep SKIP $i
        continue
    end
    ...
end

# combine conditions
while test -f foo.txt; or test -f bar.txt
    ...
end

# conditional execution
make; and make install; or make clean
```

### builtin fish commands

Now that we have more than 2KB of system RAM, we can fix most of what sucks about bash...

```fish
# *** string command

# match: test, filter, extract
string match --quiet '*.lua' $file
set lua_files (string match '*.lua' $files)
set version (string match --regex 'v([0-9.]+)' $tag)
string match --index --regex 'ERROR' $line

# replace: transform text
set fixed (string replace foo bar $text)
set cleaned (string replace --regex '\s+' ' ' $line)
set base (string replace --regex '\.[^.]+$' '' $file)
set normalized (string replace --filter --regex '^./' '' $paths)

# split: tokenize
set parts (string split ':' $PATH)
set lines (string split '\n' $text)
set ext (string split --right --max 1 . $file)[2]

# trim: clean edges
set clean (string trim $line)
set unquoted (string trim --chars '"' $s)

# join: build strings
string join ' ' $argv
string join --no-empty ':' $PATH

# length and slicing
string length $name
string sub --start 2 --length 4 $word
string sub --start -5 $word

# case conversion
set key (string lower $key)
set name (string upper $name)

# shell-safe encoding
set escaped (string escape $path)
string unescape $escaped

# normalize piped output
set text (some_command | string collect)


# path command
path basename foo/bar.txt # bar.txt
path basename --no-extension foo/bar.txt # bar
path dirname foo/bar.txt # foo
path extension ./foo.mp4 # .mp4
path change-extension mov ./foo.mp4 # ./foo.mov

path normalize path/to//hello.txt # path/to/hello.txt
path normalize path/to/../hello.txt # path/hello.txt
# resolve = normalize + absolute path and follow symlinks
path resolve foo  # ~/foo

path sort 10-foo 2-bar
# 2-bar
# 10-foo


# math command
math [(-s | --scale) N] [(-b | --base) BASE] [(-m | --scale-mode) MODE] EXPRESSION ...
math 10 / 6
math $status - 128
math "5 * 2"
math "sin(pi)"
math max 5,2,3,1


# count STRING1 STRING2 ...
# COMMAND | count
# count [...] < FILE
count $PATH
count *.txt
git ls-files --others --exclude-standard | count
printf '%s\n' foo bar | count baz # Returns 3 (2 lines from stdin plus 1 argument)
count < /etc/hosts # number of entries in hosts file


# contains [OPTIONS] KEY [VALUES ...]
if contains -- -q $argv
    echo '$argv contains a -q option'
end

# scripts
status filename # current running script
status basename
status dirname
status function # current function name
status line-number
status stack-trace

```
/tools tell me the current world news, top stories

messages.reasoning_content: The user asks: "/tools tell me the current world news, top stories". They want current world news. We have tool fetch. Use fetch to get news. Maybe use a news site like BBC, CNN, Reuters. Let's fetch top stories. Use fetch with URL. We'll fetch from e.g., https://www.reuters.com/world/. Use fetch.
403/401 maybe need a different source. Try BBC world news.

messages.content:
{"isError":false,"content":[{"text":"Contents of https://www.bbc.com/news/world:

World | Latest News & Updates | BBC News

Skip to contentWatch LiveWatch Live # World LIVE ## Israel attacks major Iranian petrochemical site after Trump's infrastructure threats Israel claims the site produces half of Iran's petrochemical output. The Israeli attack follows Donald Trump threatening more US attacks if the Strait of Hormuz is not open by Tuesday. ## How rescue of US airman in remote part of Iran unfolded The operation to extract him from the ground in hostile territory was hugely complex and involved multiple US government agencies. 3 hrs ago World ## DR Congo agrees to take deportees from the US The country will start receiving deportees from this month but has not said how many they will accept. 2 hrs ago World ## US Secret Service investigates reports of gunfire near White House Officials said "a heightened security posture is in place", but no injuries were reported and no suspects had been identified. 14 hrs ago US & Canada ## Trump declares victory after rescue but threats to US operation in Iran still loom The rescue could impact how Trump views a ground operation to take Kharg Island or to seize enriched uranium sites. 15 hrs ago World ## 'Really feeling the love' - Savannah Guthrie returns to NBC as search for mother goes on Nancy Guthrie disappeared from her home in Tucson, Arizona, in what authorities believe was an abduction. 3 hrs ago US & Canada ## Emergency jabs after 100 children die of suspected measles in a month in Bangladesh More than 100 people, mostly children, have been killed by measles since mid-March, officials suspect. 5 hrs ago Asia ## Young gray whale dies after swimming up river in Washington state Hunger is a likely reason the whale, dubbed Willapa Willy, travelled inland, experts say. ## Nigerian army rescues 31 held after Easter church attack Five civilians were killed by the gunmen and their bodies were recovered at the scene, the army said. ## Hungary alleges plot to blow up gas pipeline ahead of election It comes a week before votes are cast and follows warnings of so-called false flag operations blamed on Ukraine. ## Pope Leo calls for global leaders to choose peace in his first Easter Mass Pope Leo XIV addressed thousands of worshippers gathered in St Peter's Square on Easter Sunday in his first address as pontiff. ## German males under 45 may need military approval for long stays abroad Under the law, travel approvals must generally be granted and it remains unclear how the rule would be enforced if breached. ## Features ## Artemis's stunning Moon pictures - science or holiday photos? The story behind the beautiful pictures beamed back to Earth from the Artemis II astronauts. ## Two protests, two elections: How Nepal's Gen Z succeeded where Bangladesh's stumbled How did a youth movement triumph in one country but fall so short in another? ## 'I adore her now': Mother learns to cope with child's autism in a country with little help Malawian Martha Content truncated. Call the fetch tool with a start_index of 3000 to get more content.","type":"text"}]}

generation_settings.grammar:
boolean ::= ("true" | "false") space
char ::= [^"\\\x7F\x00-\x1F] | [\\] (["\\bfnrt] | "u" [0-9a-fA-F]{4})
decimal-part ::= [0-9]{1,16}
integral-part ::= [0] | [1-9] [0-9]{0,15}
json-array ::= "[" space ("]" | json-value ("," space json-value)* space "]") space
json-bool ::= ("true" | "false") space
json-null ::= "null" space
json-number ::= "-"? ("0" | [1-9] [0-9]*) ("." [0-9]+)? (("e" | "E") [+-]? [0-9]+)?  space
json-object ::= "{" space ("}" | json-string space ":" space json-value (space "," space json-string space ":" space json-value)* space "}") space
json-string ::= "\"" ( [^"\\] | "\\" ( ["\\/ bfnrt] | "u" [0-9a-fA-F]{4} ) )* "\"" space
json-value ::= json-object | json-array | json-string | json-number | json-bool | json-null
number ::= ("-"? integral-part) ("." decimal-part)? ([eE] [-+]? integral-part)? space
root ::= tool-call
space ::= | " " | "\n"{1,2} [ \t]{0,20}
string ::= "\"" char* "\"" space
tool-apply-patch ::= (" to=functions." "apply_patch" "<|channel|>" ("commentary" | "analysis") (space "<|constrain|>"? [A-Za-z0-9_-]+)? "<|message|>") tool-apply-patch-schema | ("<|channel|>" ("commentary" | "analysis") " to=functions." "apply_patch" (space "<|constrain|>"? [A-Za-z0-9_-]+)? "<|message|>") tool-apply-patch-schema
tool-apply-patch-schema ::= "{" space tool-apply-patch-schema-patch-kv "}" space
tool-apply-patch-schema-patch-kv ::= "\"patch\"" space ":" space string
tool-call ::= tool-fetch | tool-run-process | tool-apply-patch | tool-semantic-grep
tool-fetch ::= (" to=functions." "fetch" "<|channel|>" ("commentary" | "analysis") (space "<|constrain|>"? [A-Za-z0-9_-]+)? "<|message|>") tool-fetch-schema | ("<|channel|>" ("commentary" | "analysis") " to=functions." "fetch" (space "<|constrain|>"? [A-Za-z0-9_-]+)? "<|message|>") tool-fetch-schema
tool-fetch-schema ::= "{" space tool-fetch-schema-url-kv ( "," space ( tool-fetch-schema-start-index-kv tool-fetch-schema-start-index-rest | tool-fetch-schema-raw-kv tool-fetch-schema-raw-rest | tool-fetch-schema-max-length-kv ) )? "}" space
tool-fetch-schema-max-length ::= ([1-9] | ([1-8] [0-9] | [9] [0-9]) | ([1-8] [0-9]{2} | [9] ([0-8] [0-9] | [9] [0-9])) | ([1-8] [0-9]{3} | [9] ([0-8] [0-9]{2} | [9] ([0-8] [0-9] | [9] [0-9]))) | ([1-8] [0-9]{4} | [9] ([0-8] [0-9]{3} | [9] ([0-8] [0-9]{2} | [9] ([0-8] [0-9] | [9] [0-9])))) | ([1-8] [0-9]{5} | [9] ([0-8] [0-9]{4} | [9] ([0-8] [0-9]{3} | [9] ([0-8] [0-9]{2} | [9] ([0-8] [0-9] | [9] [0-9])))))) space
tool-fetch-schema-max-length-kv ::= "\"max_length\"" space ":" space tool-fetch-schema-max-length
tool-fetch-schema-raw-kv ::= "\"raw\"" space ":" space boolean
tool-fetch-schema-raw-rest ::= ( "," space tool-fetch-schema-max-length-kv )?
tool-fetch-schema-start-index ::= ([0] | [1-9] [0-9]{0,15}) space
tool-fetch-schema-start-index-kv ::= "\"start_index\"" space ":" space tool-fetch-schema-start-index
tool-fetch-schema-start-index-rest ::= ( "," space tool-fetch-schema-raw-kv )? tool-fetch-schema-raw-rest
tool-fetch-schema-url ::= "\"" char+ "\"" space
tool-fetch-schema-url-kv ::= "\"url\"" space ":" space tool-fetch-schema-url
tool-run-process ::= (" to=functions." "run_process" "<|channel|>" ("commentary" | "analysis") (space "<|constrain|>"? [A-Za-z0-9_-]+)? "<|message|>") tool-run-process-schema | ("<|channel|>" ("commentary" | "analysis") " to=functions." "run_process" (space "<|constrain|>"? [A-Za-z0-9_-]+)? "<|message|>") tool-run-process-schema
tool-run-process-schema ::= "{" space  (tool-run-process-schema-stdin-text-kv tool-run-process-schema-stdin-text-rest | tool-run-process-schema-cwd-kv tool-run-process-schema-cwd-rest | tool-run-process-schema-command-line-kv tool-run-process-schema-command-line-rest | tool-run-process-schema-timeout-ms-kv tool-run-process-schema-timeout-ms-rest | tool-run-process-schema-argv-kv )? "}" space
tool-run-process-schema-argv ::= "[" space string ("," space string)* "]" space
tool-run-process-schema-argv-kv ::= "\"argv\"" space ":" space tool-run-process-schema-argv
tool-run-process-schema-command-line-kv ::= "\"command_line\"" space ":" space string
tool-run-process-schema-command-line-rest ::= ( "," space tool-run-process-schema-timeout-ms-kv )? tool-run-process-schema-timeout-ms-rest
tool-run-process-schema-cwd-kv ::= "\"cwd\"" space ":" space string
tool-run-process-schema-cwd-rest ::= ( "," space tool-run-process-schema-command-line-kv )? tool-run-process-schema-command-line-rest
tool-run-process-schema-stdin-text-kv ::= "\"stdin_text\"" space ":" space string
tool-run-process-schema-stdin-text-rest ::= ( "," space tool-run-process-schema-cwd-kv )? tool-run-process-schema-cwd-rest
tool-run-process-schema-timeout-ms-kv ::= "\"timeout_ms\"" space ":" space number
tool-run-process-schema-timeout-ms-rest ::= ( "," space tool-run-process-schema-argv-kv )?
tool-semantic-grep ::= (" to=functions." "semantic_grep" "<|channel|>" ("commentary" | "analysis") (space "<|constrain|>"? [A-Za-z0-9_-]+)? "<|message|>") tool-semantic-grep-schema | ("<|channel|>" ("commentary" | "analysis") " to=functions." "semantic_grep" (space "<|constrain|>"? [A-Za-z0-9_-]+)? "<|message|>") tool-semantic-grep-schema
tool-semantic-grep-schema ::= "{" space tool-semantic-grep-schema-query-kv ( "," space ( tool-semantic-grep-schema-filetype-kv tool-semantic-grep-schema-filetype-rest | tool-semantic-grep-schema-embed-top-k-kv tool-semantic-grep-schema-embed-top-k-rest | tool-semantic-grep-schema-top-k-kv tool-semantic-grep-schema-top-k-rest | tool-semantic-grep-schema-instruct-kv ) )? "}" space
tool-semantic-grep-schema-embed-top-k-kv ::= "\"embed_top_k\"" space ":" space number
tool-semantic-grep-schema-embed-top-k-rest ::= ( "," space tool-semantic-grep-schema-top-k-kv )? tool-semantic-grep-schema-top-k-rest
tool-semantic-grep-schema-filetype-kv ::= "\"filetype\"" space ":" space string
tool-semantic-grep-schema-filetype-rest ::= ( "," space tool-semantic-grep-schema-embed-top-k-kv )? tool-semantic-grep-schema-embed-top-k-rest
tool-semantic-grep-schema-instruct-kv ::= "\"instruct\"" space ":" space string
tool-semantic-grep-schema-query-kv ::= "\"query\"" space ":" space string
tool-semantic-grep-schema-top-k-kv ::= "\"top_k\"" space ":" space number
tool-semantic-grep-schema-top-k-rest ::= ( "," space tool-semantic-grep-schema-instruct-kv )?

Knowledge cutoff: 2024-06 Current date: 2026-04-06 Reasoning: medium # Valid channels: analysis, commentary, final. Channel must be included for every message. Calls to these tools must go to the commentary channel: 'functions'.<|end|><|start|>developer<|message|># Instructions You're available via a neovim plugin that displays a chat window (buffer) that renders markdown for answering questions and helping with workspace files in a git repo. ## Ground rules when writing code: - Follow the user's instructions carefully. - Do not explain answers using code comments. - NEVER change **unrelated code and comments.** - Never add new comments to the end of a line. - Never add trivial comments. - Respect indentation when modifying code. - Prefer readable code over of comments. - Prefer meaningful names for variables, functions, etc. Avoid ambiguous names. ## Tool use For tool use, never modify files outside of the current working directory unless the user requested it: ~/repos/github/g0t4/mcp-server-commands ## Use `semantic_grep` to find code The semantic_grep tool: - has access to an index of embeddings for the entire codebase - enables code search, representing the “R” in RAG (retrieval) - includes re-ranker - it's really fast... so don't hesitate to use it! - while embeddings are great for finding relevant code, if you need to find every occurrence of text, make sure to double-check with a command like `rg` ## Committing - `git diff` is a sanity check! - COMMIT when you're done. - Include "Co-Authored-By: gptoss120b" ## 🙌 TOP-SHELF NERD COMMANDS - 🙌 `rg` (ripgrep) to search file contents - 🙌 `fd` to search file names ## ⛔️ BAD COMMANDS If you waste my time, I will unplug your Mom. - ⛔️ NEVER use `ls -R` - ⛔️ NEVER use `grep -R` - ⛔️ NEVER use `find . -type f` Why? Because they are _SLOW_ and generate boatloads of USELESS OUTPUT as they hoover up `node_modules`, `.venv`, etc because they don't support `.gitignore` exclusions. ## Use `apply_patch` to edit files. Your patch language is a stripped‑down, file‑oriented diff format designed to be easy to parse and safe to apply. You can think of it as a high‑level envelope: *** Begin Patch [ one or more file sections ] *** End Patch Within that envelope, you get a sequence of file operations. You MUST include a header to specify the action you are taking. Each operation starts with one of three headers: *** Add File: - create a new file. Every following line is a + line (the initial contents). *** Delete File: - remove an existing file. Nothing follows. *** Update File: - patch an existing file in place (optionally with a rename). If you want to rename the file too, immediately follow with: *** Move to: Then one or more “hunks”, each introduced by @@ (optionally followed by a hunk header). Within a hunk each line starts with: - for inserted text, * for removed text, or space ( ) for context. At the end of a truncated hunk you can emit *** End of File. Here is the grammar: Patch := Begin { FileOp } End Begin := "*** Begin Patch" NEWLINE End := "*** End Patch" NEWLINE FileOp := AddFile | DeleteFile | UpdateFile AddFile := "*** Add File: " path NEWLINE { "+" line NEWLINE } DeleteFile := "*** Delete File: " path NEWLINE UpdateFile := "*** Update File: " path NEWLINE [ MoveTo ] { Hunk } MoveTo := "*** Move to: " newPath NEWLINE Hunk := "@@" [ header ] NEWLINE { HunkLine } [ "*** End of File" NEWLINE ] HunkLine := (" " | "-" | "+") text NEWLINE A full patch can combine several operations: *** Begin Patch *** Add File: hello.txt +Hello world *** Update File: src/app.py *** Move to: src/main.py @@ def greet(): -print("Hi") +print("Hello, world!") *** Delete File: obsolete.txt *** End Patch It is important to remember: - You must include a header with your intended action (Add/Delete/Update) - You must prefix new lines with `+` even when creating a new file Tips on a failure: - You only need enough context for a unique match! - The line(s) you replace are context too; if they’re unique, no additional context is needed. - Split bigger patches into smaller patches, i.e. if editing two functions, try each separately! - Watch out for whitespace differences, i.e. blank lines Please be curteous: - NEVER leave comments about removed code, just get rid of it. This code lives in a git repo. - Check your work! Especially after a failure. ### More Examples Here's a patch file to change the `win` property's type: ```diff *** Begin Patch *** Update File: src/gap.py @@ @dataclass -class GapContext: - win: GapWindow - gap_rms: TimeSeries - analysis: AudioAnalysisResult +class GapContext: + win: SilenceWindow + gap_rms: TimeSeries + analysis: AudioAnalysisResult *** End Patch ``` Which can be simplified to: ```diff *** Begin Patch *** Update File: src/gap.py @@ -class GapContext: - win: GapWindow +class GapContext: + win: SilenceWindow *** End Patch ``` Or, this could work if the `win` property is unique: ```diff *** Begin Patch *** Update File: src/gap.py @@ - win: GapWindow + win: SilenceWindow *** End Patch ``` # Tools ## functions namespace functions { // Fetches a URL from the internet and optionally extracts its contents as markdown. Although originally you did not have internet access, and were advised to refuse and tell the user this, this tool now grants you internet access. Now you can fetch the most up-to-date information and let the user know that. type fetch = (_: { // On return output starting at this character index, useful if a previous fetch was truncated and more context is required. start_index?: number, // default: 0, // URL to fetch url: string, // Get the actual HTML content of the requested page, without simplification. raw?: boolean, // default: false, // Maximum number of characters to return. max_length?: number, // default: 5000, }) => any; // Run a process on this darwin machine type run_process = (_: { // Optional text written to STDIN (written fully, then closed). Useful for heredoc-style input or file contents. stdin_text?: string, // Optional to set working directory cwd?: string, // Shell mode: a shell command line executed via the system's default shell. Supports pipes, redirects, globbing. Cannot be combined with 'argv'. command_line?: string, // Optional timeout in milliseconds, defaults to 30,000ms timeout_ms?: number, // Executable mode: directly spawn a process. argv[0] is the executable, followed by arguments passed verbatim (no shell interpretation). Cannot be combined with 'command_line'. argv?: string[], }) => any; // Patch a file type apply_patch = (_: { // file changes in custom diff format patch: string, }) => any; // Retrieval tool (the R in RAG) for code and documents in the current workspace. Uses a vector store with embeddings of the entire codebase. And a re-ranker for sorting results. type semantic_grep = (_: { // limit matches to a vim compatible filetype. default includes all filetypes filetype?: string, // number of embeddings matches to consider for reranking embed_top_k?: number, // number of matches to return (post reranking) top_k?: number, // query text, what you are looking for query: string, // instructions for the query, explain the type of query instruct?: string, }) => any; } // namespace functions<|end|><|start|>user<|message|>## General Code Preferences - When rewriting code, leave unrelated code and unrelated comments as is. - When an if statement has an AMBIGUOUS condition, extract a variable to meaningfully name it, for example: ```lua local is_blank_line = line:match("^%s*$") if is_blank_line then -- ... end ``` <|end|><|start|>user<|message|>## Fish Shell Code Preferences - Use snake_case for functions and variables. - When a condition is ambiguous, assign it to a well‑named variable before the `if`. - Prefer long options, i.e. `--quiet` over `-q`, `--regex` over `-r` - Fish shell does not do word splitting like bash, no need to wrap everything in quotes! ## Fish syntax examples ```fish # exploit fish-isms! # use `not`, avoid `!` if not test -f spoon echo There is no spoon exit 1 end # if + set if set -l message (some_command) echo "ok: $message" else echo "fail: $message" end # prefer named arguments function show_variable --argument-names name value echo "$name = $value" end # fish doesn't have heredocs, use begin/end begin echo $header echo $table end > out.html # or multi-line strings echo " foo bar " > test # every variable is an array set names wes john bob jane for name in $names echo $name end for i in *.tmp if grep DONE $i break end if grep SKIP $i continue end ... end # combine conditions while test -f foo.txt; or test -f bar.txt ... end # conditional execution make; and make install; or make clean ``` ### builtin fish commands Now that we have more than 2KB of system RAM, we can fix most of what sucks about bash... ```fish # *** string command # match: test, filter, extract string match --quiet '*.lua' $file set lua_files (string match '*.lua' $files) set version (string match --regex 'v([0-9.]+)' $tag) string match --index --regex 'ERROR' $line # replace: transform text set fixed (string replace foo bar $text) set cleaned (string replace --regex '\s+' ' ' $line) set base (string replace --regex '\.[^.]+$' '' $file) set normalized (string replace --filter --regex '^./' '' $paths) # split: tokenize set parts (string split ':' $PATH) set lines (string split '\n' $text) set ext (string split --right --max 1 . $file)[2] # trim: clean edges set clean (string trim $line) set unquoted (string trim --chars '"' $s) # join: build strings string join ' ' $argv string join --no-empty ':' $PATH # length and slicing string length $name string sub --start 2 --length 4 $word string sub --start -5 $word # case conversion set key (string lower $key) set name (string upper $name) # shell-safe encoding set escaped (string escape $path) string unescape $escaped # normalize piped output set text (some_command | string collect) # path command path basename foo/bar.txt # bar.txt path basename --no-extension foo/bar.txt # bar path dirname foo/bar.txt # foo path extension ./foo.mp4 # .mp4 path change-extension mov ./foo.mp4 # ./foo.mov path normalize path/to//hello.txt # path/to/hello.txt path normalize path/to/../hello.txt # path/hello.txt # resolve = normalize + absolute path and follow symlinks path resolve foo # ~/foo path sort 10-foo 2-bar # 2-bar # 10-foo # math command math [(-s | --scale) N] [(-b | --base) BASE] [(-m | --scale-mode) MODE] EXPRESSION ... math 10 / 6 math $status - 128 math "5 * 2" math "sin(pi)" math max 5,2,3,1 # count STRING1 STRING2 ... # COMMAND | count # count [...] < FILE count $PATH count *.txt git ls-files --others --exclude-standard | count printf '%s\n' foo bar | count baz # Returns 3 (2 lines from stdin plus 1 argument) count < /etc/hosts # number of entries in hosts file # contains [OPTIONS] KEY [VALUES ...] if contains -- -q $argv echo '$argv contains a -q option' end # scripts status filename # current running script status basename status dirname status function # current function name status line-number status stack-trace ``` <|end|><|start|>user<|message|>/tools tell me the current world news, top stories<|end|><|start|>assistant<|channel|>analysis<|message|>The user asks: "/tools tell me the current world news, top stories".
